textPlain: The name gets me every single time. Always think it’s going to be about radio LoRa

Can someone explain the bit counting argument in the reinforcement learning part?I don’t get why a trajectory would provide only one bit of information.Each step of the trajectory is at least giving information about what state transitions are possible.An infinitely long trajectory can explore the whole state space if there are no absorbing states. Such a trajectory would provide a massive amount of information about the system, even if we ignored the final reward.
Took me a moment to realize this is not about LoRa.
Thinking Machines have put out a string of incredibly high-quality posts lately. Hard to oversell how much cred it's buying them with the AI research community! Keep up the great work folks
Dang it! Got me too! I've been wanting to hop into Meshtastic lately.
I thought it was Lora the CRTD implementation, but then realized that Loro
Yeah, kinda disappointed it's just more AI stuff...
